steps.title: Use the perimeter
steps.content: Since the perimeter is the sum of the three side lengths, subtract the two known congruent sides from the total perimeter to find the third side.

steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation
steps.content: In an isosceles triangle, perimeter equals the sum of all three sides. With two congruent sides of 24 feet each and total perimeter of 84 feet: $$24 + 24 + x = 84$$ where $x$ is the unknown third side.

steps.title: Solve for the third side
steps.content: $$48 + x = 84$$ $$x = 36$$ That matches the correct answer - C is correct.
